parsed_review.summary: CP00-883 is a descriptor-only RP29 Commercial Readiness P05 fixture/golden-case tail pack (40 units, risk B, RP29.P05.M02.S18-RP29.P05.M04.S15). The pack-level claim is descriptor-only / no-write / no-real-data / no-runtime with production_ready=false and runtime_ready=false; it does NOT assert runtime or enterprise trust. Verified support: (1) Implementation (registry.js CP883 builders, ui.js matrices, validators.js) enforces descriptor_only=true, runtime_execution=false, full no_write_attestation, no-count-leak, stable-ID no customer/Matter exposure, replay-no-runtime, and missing-test-prompt read-only; fixtures are synthetic-only (tenant-synthetic-*, matter-synthetic-*) with no credentials/secrets/real client data. (2) Coverage matches baseline exactly: 40 units, deliverable distribution test5/impl14/fixture14/claude_review2/security_audit4/hermes_evidence1, micro-title 3/22/15, and row titles reconcile (M02 slice17-20, M03 full22, M04 slice0-15). (3) Tests (model.test.js/service.test.js) assert empty coverage/descriptor/contract error arrays plus every boundary flag. (4) Contract carries validation.valid=true with complete no_write_attestation and latest_pack CP00-883; compatibility alias and validate script chain coverage->descriptor->contract->capability/gate checks; index.js re-exports the imported symbols. (5) Authority boundary holds: Claude read_only, promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false, claude_is_final_approval=false; Hermes packets evidence_only with emits_runtime_receipt=false (control-plane review/receipt only, not product runtime). (6) Review hardening: auth preflight passed with valid 1444-byte JSON; no auth failure, 0-byte, malformed, fenced, or tool-shaped review evidence is being passed off — the pending_review placeholders in adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json, and claude-review-result.json are the expected pre-gating state for this very review, and command-evidence shows the initial validation (rp29:commercial:validate, model tests) at exit 0 with the full deterministic ladder scheduled post-review/pre-commit. Only one cosmetic P3 was found (stale RP29.P04.M04 phase reference in the shared UI surface descriptor text); it is inert, unvalidated, and has no boundary or functional impact. No P0/P1/P2. blocks_goal_closeout=false per instruction scope (this CP closeout only; the human/enterprise-trust approval authority boundary is outside this receipt and the pack makes no such claim). Recommend promoting the descriptor-only / no-write / no-real-data boundaries.

findings.title: Stale RP29.P04.M04 phase reference in shared UI surface descriptor text
findings.details: createCommercialUiSurfaceMatrix().closeout_handoff.next_ui_rows reads 'continue RP29.P04.M04 fixture, build, Hermes, Claude, handoff, snapshot, and leak rows', referencing P04 while this pack operates in RP29.P05.M02-M04. The pack-specific handoff (createCommercialCp883CloseoutHandoff) and the fixture/golden matrix handoff (createCommercialUiFixtureGoldenCaseMatrix().closeout_handoff -> 'continue RP29.P05 fixture/golden-case rows') are correct, so this is leftover generic text in the shared surface descriptor (likely carried from the CP881/CP882 P04 era). The string is frozen descriptor metadata, is not asserted by any validator, and has no runtime, no-write, no-real-data, or production_ready impact.
findings.recommendation: Optional, non-blocking: update next_ui_rows in the shared UI surface matrix to reference the current RP29.P05 phase (or make it phase-agnostic) in a future commercial pack. No change required to close CP00-883.
findings.post_review_disposition: Non-blocking cosmetic descriptor text; does not affect any boundary or the production_ready=false claim. Optional cleanup in a later commercial pack.
